title: University of Chicago Press Workers Vote to Unionize
summary: University of Chicago Press Workers Vote to Unionize
imageAlt: University of Chicago Press Workers Vote to Unionize
aiSummary: University of Chicago Press Workers Vote to Unionize

whyMatters: Noticia de actualidad en el sector de cultura.

multimedia.alt: University of Chicago Press Workers Vote to Unionize

fullArticle:
Eighty-nine percent of University of Chicago Press workers voted in favor of unionizing with the Chicago News Guild TNG-CWA Local 34071 and will soon begin bargaining for their first contract, according to an announcement Wednesday.

The UCP Workers Guild is the first union in the publisher’s 130-year history and comprises 134 onsite and hybrid staffers across “most departments," per the release, including certain UCP distribution center workers. Other distribution center workers are organized with the Teamsters, and will retain that affiliation.

“Following certification of the election results by the NLRB, the University will bargain in good faith with the UCP Workers Guild,” the press wrote to PW in a statement. “We greatly value the work of UChicago Press employees and their important ...
